speaker: Donald J. Trump
quote: We've never had anywhere near $18 trillion invested in our country, neither has any other country.
venue: Whole Milk in Schools Bill Signing
claim_text: Trump said nearly $18 trillion had been invested in the U.S. and no country had seen anything comparable.

sources.publisher: BEA - Direct Investment by Country and Industry, 2024
BEA - New Foreign Direct Investment in the United States, 2024
Federal Reserve FRED - Gross Private Domestic Investment (GPDI)

assessment.rationale:
Evidence:
- The transcript quote says nearly $18 trillion had been invested in the U.S., and that no country had seen anything comparable.
- BEA reports the foreign direct investment position in the United States at end-2024 was $5.71 trillion.
- BEA reports new foreign direct investment expenditures in 2024 were $151.0 billion, with planned total expenditures of $157.0 billion.
- FRED series GPDI (source: BEA) shows U.S. gross private domestic investment around $5.52 trillion annual rate in Q4 2025.

Why This Is False:
- The claim presents an enormous concrete total ($18 trillion) as incoming/invested fact.
- Official U.S. investment aggregates and FDI position data are far below that figure for the referenced period.
- The statement appears to conflate announced intentions with measured investment and does not define a verifiable scope/timeframe for the $18 trillion number.

Shut Down False Argument:
- Press-release announcements are not the same thing as recorded investment in national accounts.
- If $18 trillion were actually invested or firmly incoming on standard definitions, it would appear in BEA benchmark totals; it does not.
- On available official records, this number is not substantiated.
